message:
fix(am): AWK stdio guard never fired — END block overrode pattern exit

The AWK guard in sync_toml_mcp_url() was meant to detect `command =`
entries (stdio transport) and skip URL injection. However, the END
block's `exit 1` always ran last, overriding the pattern block's
`exit 0`. This meant the guard never triggered, allowing `url =` to
be injected into working stdio configs (e.g., Codex TOML).

Fix: use a `found` variable set by the pattern block and check it in
the END block, so the exit code correctly reflects whether `command =`
was found.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
